seo_title: Current Time in Oklahoma City — CST/CDT
seo_description: Current time in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma. Central Time Zone — CST (UTC-6) in winter, CDT (UTC-5) during DST. Live clock, time differences for Oklahoma City, OK.

faq.question: What time is it in Oklahoma City right now?
faq.answer: Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, USA uses Central Standard Time (CST, UTC-6) in winter and Central Daylight Time (CDT, UTC-5) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/Chicago. Oklahoma City is on the same time as Dallas, Chicago, Houston, and Kansas City — 1 hour behind New York and 1 hour ahead of Denver. The live clock above shows the current Oklahoma City time.

faq.question: What time zone is Oklahoma City in?
faq.answer: Oklahoma City, Oklahoma is in the Central Time Zone (CT). It uses Central Standard Time (CST, UTC-6) from the first Sunday in November to the second Sunday in March, and Central Daylight Time (CDT, UTC-5) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/Chicago. Oklahoma City shares its time zone with Dallas, Chicago, Houston, and Minneapolis.

faq.question: Does Oklahoma City observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Oklahoma City, Oklahoma observes Daylight Saving Time. Clocks spring forward 1 hour on the second Sunday in March at 2:00 AM (CST → CDT) and fall back on the first Sunday in November. Oklahoma follows the standard US DST schedule.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Oklahoma City and New York?
faq.answer: Oklahoma City (CST/CDT, Central Time) is always 1 hour behind New York (EST/EDT, Eastern Time). Both cities observe DST on the same schedule, keeping the gap constant year-round. When it is 9:00 AM in New York, it is 8:00 AM in Oklahoma City.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Oklahoma City and Denver?
faq.answer: Oklahoma City (CST/CDT, Central Time) is always 1 hour ahead of Denver (MST/MDT, Mountain Time). Both cities observe DST on the same schedule, keeping the 1-hour gap constant year-round. When it is 9:00 AM in Denver, it is 10:00 AM in Oklahoma City.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Oklahoma City and Los Angeles?
faq.answer: Oklahoma City (CST/CDT, Central Time) is always 2 hours ahead of Los Angeles (PST/PDT, Pacific Time). Both observe DST on the same schedule, so the 2-hour gap is constant year-round. When it is 9:00 AM in Los Angeles, it is 11:00 AM in Oklahoma City.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Oklahoma City?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Oklahoma City are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM CT, Monday through Friday. Oklahoma City is the state capital and a major energy industry hub — home to Devon Energy, OGE Energy, and many oil and gas companies. The Oklahoma City Stock Exchange (now merged) and energy corridor offices generally follow Central Time business hours. The Bricktown entertainment district operates from 11:00 AM–10:00 PM or later daily.
